subject: Social Science
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze market operations and buyer-seller interactions
Investigate price variations and factors affecting them
Compare traditional, modern, and digital markets
Trace supply chains of products from producer to consumer
Develop research, analytical, and presentation skills

lesson_plan.title: Market Research Introduction
topics_covered: Introduction to market research and observation methods
teaching_strategy: Show videos or case studies of market activities
student_activities: Select a local market for detailed study
Plan observation of goods, services, and layout
List questions to investigate during visit

lesson_plan.title: Market Visit and Data Collection
topics_covered: Observation of goods, services, and pricing
teaching_strategy: Guide students on systematic data collection
student_activities: Visit selected market or study via online resources if needed
Record prices, types of goods/services, and crowd patterns
Note any bargaining or trade techniques observed

lesson_plan.title: Price Analysis and Trends
topics_covered: Factors affecting price changes (supply, demand, seasonality)
Comparison of prices across shops and markets
teaching_strategy: Demonstrate data analysis using charts or tables
Ask students to identify patterns in collected data
student_activities: Organize data into tables and graphs
Analyze why prices differ for same goods
Identify trends in seasonal or weekly price changes

lesson_plan.title: Supply Chain Study
topics_covered: Journey of a product from producer to consumer
Role of wholesalers, retailers, and transport
teaching_strategy: Show examples of food or clothing products
student_activities: Select one product and trace its supply chain
Create a detailed flowchart showing each step
Identify challenges and efficiencies in the chain

lesson_plan.title: Comparing Market Types
topics_covered: Traditional vs modern vs digital markets
Advantages and disadvantages of each type
teaching_strategy: Discuss examples of supermarkets, weekly haats, and online markets
Encourage critical thinking about accessibility, pricing, and convenience
student_activities: Prepare a comparison table of three market types
Discuss which market suits different products and customers

lesson_plan.title: Market Presentation and Reflection
topics_covered: Present analysis of market research, pricing, supply chains, and market types
teaching_strategy: Encourage use of charts, tables, and visuals
student_activities: Prepare a detailed report or digital slides
Reflect on how markets influence daily life and economy

teaching_aids: Pictures, charts, and videos of markets
Templates for data collection and analysis
Notebooks, pens, and digital tools for presentations
teacher_tip: Encourage students to observe markets critically and collect real data. Use graphs, flowcharts, and comparison tables to develop analytical thinking. Connect lessons to real-life shopping and economic decisions.
